title: versionInfo.APIVersion wrong type in ReplyGetVersion
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Building the project seems to give some errors in the varlink API.

```
λ libpod » make BUILDTAGS='seccomp varlink systemd'
# Only generate the varlink code on Linux (see issue #4814).
go generate ./pkg/varlink/...
go build -mod=vendor  -gcflags 'all=-trimpath=/home/fox/Git/prosjekter/Go/libpod' -asmflags 'all=-trimpath=/home/fox/Git/prosjekter/Go/libpod' -ldflags '-X github.com/containers/podman/v2/libpod/define.gitCommit=d8868746fd3f45b9bfd79e37bf2aa0c7103245fd-dirty -X github.com/containers/podman/v2/libpod/define.buildInfo=1600811562 -X github.com/containers/podman/v2/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/podman/v2/libpod/config._etcDir=/etc ' -tags "seccomp varlink systemd" -o bin/podman ./cmd/podman
# github.com/containers/podman/v2/pkg/varlinkapi
pkg/varlinkapi/system.go:31:14: cannot use versionInfo.APIVersion (type string) as type int64 in argument to call.ReplyGetVersion
make: *** [Makefile:186: bin/podman] Error 2
```

It's unclear to me if this is a build system issue or a mistake in the API usage.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `git clone`

2. `git checkout v2.1.0`

3. `make BUILDTAGS='seccomp varlink systemd'`

**Describe the results you received:**

A failed build

**Describe the results you expected:**

A successful build

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Go version is `1.15.2`, but I find it hard to believe it's related to this issue.

comments.body: Ah, apologies. I didn't search the correct thing and it seems like #7734 fixes it.
